examples.title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm
examples.description: Standard midpoint circle algorithm for raster display
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MidpointCircle(r)
BEGIN
 x ← 0
 y ← r
 P ← 1 - r
 
 WHILE x < y DO
 Plot points in 8 octants:
 (x, y), (y, x), (y, -x), (x, -y)
 (-x, -y), (-y, -x), (-y, x), (-x, y)
 
 IF P < 0 THEN
 // Midpoint is inside circle
 x ← x + 1
 P ← P + 2*x + 1
 ELSE
 // Midpoint is outside/on circle
 x ← x + 1
 y ← y - 1
 P ← P + 2*x - 2*y + 1
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm starts at (0,r) and uses decision parameter P to choose between East and Southeast pixels. When P<0, the midpoint is inside so we choose East; otherwise choose Southeast. Each computed point is plotted in all 8 octants due to symmetry.

examples.title: Polar Circle Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PolarCircle(r)
BEGIN
 angle ← 0
 angleStep ← 1/r
 
 WHILE angle ≤ π/4 DO
 x ← round(r * cos(angle))
 y ← round(r * sin(angle))
 
 // Plot in all 8 octants
 Plot points:
 (x, y), (y, x), (y, -x), (x, -y)
 (-x, -y), (-y, -x), (-y, x), (-x, y)
 
 angle ← angle + angleStep
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This method samples angles from 0 to 45° (π/4 radians) and computes x and y using trigonometric functions. The angle step is chosen to ensure consecutive points are approximately one pixel apart. Due to 8-way symmetry, points are reflected to all octants.

examples.title: Bresenham's Circle Algorithm (Simplified)
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BresenhamCircle(r)
BEGIN
 x ← 0
 y ← r
 d ← 2 * (1 - r)
 
 WHILE x < y DO
 Plot pixel (x, y) and symmetric points
 
 IF d < 0 THEN
 // Choose E
 d ← d + 2*x + 3
 x ← x + 1
 ELSE
 // Choose SE
 d ← d + 2*(x - y) + 5
 x ← x + 1
 y ← y - 1
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Uses variable 'd' as decision parameter. When d<0, the error is small so choose East pixel; when d≥0, choose Southeast pixel. The decision parameter is updated incrementally without recalculating squares.
